name: Family possessions, medium
description: A 3d6 biography table of middling household rights, standing, expectations, and claims that do not overlap with item rewards.

results.description: A lease that looked respectable from the road and fragile in any conversation involving money.
A market right, stall claim, or place of trade known to the town even when paperwork went missing.
Trade credit extended on the strength of your household name, useful until misfortune made everyone suddenly precise.
A modest but real expectation of decent marriage, treated in the family almost as an asset.
A set of clients or customers who preferred your household by habit and would rather not begin again elsewhere.
Access to storage, workshop space, or carriage room that made the family more settled than its rivals.
A favorable tenancy or contract renewed often enough to feel inherited.
A household reputation for respectable dealing, careful speech, and remembering obligations.
Partnership rights or a small share in an enterprise that brought influence even when it brought little coin.
Priority in local arrangements: first call on work, first notice of opportunities, or first hearing in disputes.
The expectation that apprentices, servants, or dependents attached themselves to your household for advancement.
A marriage connection under discussion, half promise and half strategy, improving how the family was treated already.
A recognized place in guild, parish, or town life that let your household speak and be answered.
Long-standing access to suppliers, carriers, or intermediaries who did not offer the same terms to strangers.
Household standing solid enough that people assumed continuity, credit, and competent management before asking questions.
The sort of family position that turned opportunity into expectation and expectation into a form of inherited property.
